name: Customer who already declined revisits the public quote — sees a DECLINED pill, no actions panel
description: Terminal-state visibility on the public quote. Once a quote is accepted (status='accepted') or declined (status='lost'), the actions panel is hidden — there's no way to switch the answer. A status pill in the header tells the customer what they did. Both terminal states render the same redacted projection with the appropriate pill.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.role: Came back to the email later
persona.context: Tapped the link a second time, possibly forwarded it to a partner.

steps.expect: Quote header shows DECLINED pill (pink/maroon, uppercase letterspaced); no Accept input, no decline form, no Ask-a-question — actions panel is null because (accepted || declined) is true
Contact-back footer still shows contractor phone/email so the customer can reach out off-platform if they changed their mind

steps.action: Tries clicking around looking for an undo
steps.expect: There is none — the design intentionally requires re-engaging via the contractor (who can re-open from /assistant)

evaluationCriteria.check: DECLINED pill matches the visual weight of the ACCEPTED pill (same size/typography), differentiated only by color
User isn't given a sketchy 'change my mind' button — preserves trust in the decline as a real signal
The redacted projection still includes the line items + total so the customer can reference what they declined
Pill text is readable by screen readers as part of the document outline; status is announced near the h1
